info.description:
The Vantaca API is a JSON based web service. Access to the API is granted by individual Vantaca customers who will grant vendors individual access to their dataset. Credentials can and will vary from one customer to another.  </br></br>Credentials will need to have an accompanying IP Address whitelisted in order to successfully access Vantaca's Standard APIs. All connections must be made using SSL. </br>  </br> **Production URL - api.vantaca.net** </br></br> 
Most /read/ methods can use the parameter **zip** as a boolean. If **true**, the JSON response will be compressed and returned as a zip file. Unless otherwise specified in each endpoint, the zip archive will contain a single file called, **data.json**. *This can reduce the bandwidth needed to process company-wide information*. The parameter is always optional but is recommended when making calls for bulk data. If **false**, the response body will be a JSON object. </br></br> Vantaca's API passes each set of credentials via Basic Authorization with the following required parameters for ***all*** endpoints: </br> - **company**  </br> - **login**  </br> - **pwd**</br></br></br>
## Changelog:
  **Version 3.8.0 (5/28/2026)**: 
  </br> - Added new endpoint for write/createActionItemNote. This new endpoint allows for adding of new notes, as well as adding attachments, without the need to step the actionItem.
  Added new endpoint for read/collectionList
  </br>
  </br>To access the most up-to-date version,  [Click Here](https://app.swaggerhub.com/apis/Vantaca/vantacaStandard/)**
  </br>
  </br>

## **Vantaca API TERMS OF USE**
</br>

Last Updated: May 2, 2025 

**1. ACCEPTANCE OF TERMS**
By accessing or using the API services provided by Vantaca ("Provider," "we," "us," or "our"), you ("User," "you," or "your") agree to be bound by these Terms of Use ("Terms"). If you are using the API on behalf of an entity, you represent that you have authority to bind that entity to these Terms, and "you" refers to that entity. 

**2. DEFINITIONS**
- "API": The application programming interface made available by Provider, including swagger based documentation. 
- "API Key": The unique authentication credential issued to you that permits access to the API. 
- "Application": Any software, website, or service you develop that interfaces with the API. 
- "Content": Any data or information accessed or retrieved through the API. 

**3. LICENSE GRANT AND RESTRICTIONS**

3.1 License 

Subject to these Terms, Provider grants you a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable, revocable license to access and use the API to develop, test, and support your Application and to integrate your Application with the features and functionality of the API. 

3.2 Restrictions 

You shall not: 

- Sell, rent, lease, sublicense, redistribute, or syndicate access to the API or API Key
- Modify, create derivative works, decompile, reverse engineer, or disassemble the API
- Use the API for any illegal purpose or in violation of any local, state, national, or international law
- Use the API to develop an Application that replicates or provides similar functionality or features as Vantaca
- Interfere with or disrupt the integrity or performance of the API or its related systems
- Attempt to gain unauthorized access to the API or related systems
- Use the API in a manner that exceeds reasonable request volume or constitutes excessive or abusive usage 
- Remove or alter any proprietary notices or marks on the API or its documentation 

**4. API USAGE AND RATE LIMITS**

4.1 Rate Limits

Provider may set and enforce limits on your use of the API in our sole discretion. These limits may include, but are not limited to, the number of API requests that may be made, the number of users you may serve, or other limitations. 

4.2 API Keys 

You are responsible for maintaining the security of your API Key and any passwords or credentials. You may not share your API Key with any third party, except for authorized agents acting on your behalf. 

4.3 Monitoring and Analytics 

Provider may monitor and collect data relating to your usage of the API to ensure compliance with these Terms and for the purposes of improving and enhancing the API. 

**5. USER OBLIGATIONS** 

5.1 Compliance 

You agree to: 

- Comply with all applicable laws and regulations 
- Comply with all provisions of these Terms and any associated documentation 
- Respect the intellectual property, privacy, and other rights of third parties 
- Promptly update your Application to install the most current version of the API when notified 

5.2 User Applications 

Your Application must: 

- Include your own terms of service and privacy policy 
- Accurately disclose the functions and features provided by the API 
- Not imply any partnership with or endorsement by Provider 

**6. DATA RIGHTS AND PRIVACY** 

6.1 Data Rights 

You retain all rights to your data submitted through the API. You grant Provider a non-exclusive license to access, use, and process your data solely to provide and maintain the API services. 

6.2 Privacy 

Provider will process any personal data in accordance with our Privacy Policy, which is incorporated by reference into these Terms. You agree to comply with all applicable privacy laws and to provide a legally adequate privacy notice to your users. 

**7. MODIFICATIONS**

7.1 API Modifications 

Provider reserves the right to modify, suspend, or discontinue the API or any part thereof at any time, with or without notice. We will use commercially reasonable efforts to notify you of material changes. 

7.2 Terms Modifications 

Provider may modify these Terms at any time by posting the modified Terms. Your continued use of the API following any modification constitutes your acceptance of the modified Terms. 

**8. TERMINATION**

8.1 Termination by User 

You may terminate these Terms by discontinuing use of the API. 

8.2 Termination by Provider 

Provider may terminate these Terms or suspend your access to the API at any time for any reason, including but not limited to, your breach of these Terms. 

8.3 Effect of Termination 

Upon termination, all licenses granted under these Terms will terminate, and you must cease all use of the API. 

**9. DISCLAIMER OF WARRANTIES** 

THE API IS PROVIDED "AS IS" AND "AS AVAILABLE." PROVIDER DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT. PROVIDER DOES NOT WARRANT THAT THE API WILL BE ERROR-FREE, UNINTERRUPTED, OR FREE FROM VIRUSES OR OTHER HARMFUL COMPONENTS. 

**10. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY**

IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO YOUR USE OF THE API. PROVIDER'S TOTAL LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THESE TERMS SHALL NOT EXCEED THE GREATER OF $100 OR THE AMOUNT PAID BY YOU TO PROVIDER IN THE SIX MONTHS PRECEDING THE EVENT GIVING RISE TO THE LIABILITY. 

**11. INDEMNIFICATION** 

You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Provider and its officers, directors, employees, agents, and affiliates from and against any and all claims, damages, obligations, losses, liabilities, costs, and expenses arising from: (a) your use of the API; (b) your violation of these Terms; (c) your violation of any third-party right; or (d) any claim that your Application caused damage to a third party. 

**12. GENERAL PROVISIONS** 

12.1 Governing Law 

These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of [Your Jurisdiction], without regard to its conflict of law principles. 

12.2 Dispute Resolution 

Any dispute arising from these Terms shall be resolved [specify dispute resolution mechanism, e.g., arbitration, specific courts, etc.]. 

12.3 Severability 

If any provision of these Terms is found to be unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect. 

12.4 No Waiver 

No failure or delay by Provider in exercising any right under these Terms shall operate as a waiver of that or any other right. 

12.5 Assignment 

You may not assign these Terms or any rights or obligations hereunder without Provider's prior written consent. Provider may assign these Terms without restriction. 

12.6 Entire Agreement 

These Terms constitute the entire agreement between you and Provider regarding the API and supersede all prior agreements and understandings, whether written or oral. 

**13. CONTACT INFORMATION** 

If you have any questions about these Terms, please contact us at VendorSupport@vantaca.com
info.title: Vantaca's Standard APIs

tags.name: /read/
tags.description: These methods read data and can be called in bulk or for single entities for more efficient processing. </br>  All calls will use the same URL path: /read/{***method***}

tags.name: /write/
tags.description: These methods write data. Unless otherwise specified, all object types available are paired with individual /read/ to allow full CRUD (Create, Read, Update, and Destory) operations against any item. Note: Entering invalid dates may result in them appearing as null or blank in Vantaca </br> All calls will use the same URL path: /write/{***method***}

tags.name: /AP/
tags.description: These methods are specific to AP or Invoice related functions and can only be accessed by designated AP credentials. Note: Entering invalid dates may result in them appearing as null or blank in Vantaca </br> All AP calls will use the same URL path: /AP/{***method***}

get.summary: retrieves a list of all ARC Request action items

parameters.name: assocCode
parameters.description: unique identifer for an association. *Will filter results to a single association.*

parameters.name: accountNo
parameters.description: unique identifier for a single homeowner account

parameters.name: xnNumber
parameters.description: unique identifier for an  arc request

parameters.name: includeMessage
parameters.description: if true, messages will be included in response, else, false

204.description: no content - call was successful, but no data for given parameters

500.description: internal error - internal processing error, please contact support if issue persists

get.summary: retrieves a list of live associations with related data

parameters.name: assocCode
parameters.description: unique identifer for an association. *Will filter results to a single association.*

parameters.name: account
parameters.description: unique identifier to return an individual homeowner account

parameters.name: propertyID
parameters.description: unique identifier to return all accounts for a single property record

parameters.name: hoid
parameters.description: unique identifier to return all accounts for a single homeowner record

parameters.name: includeCharges
parameters.description: if **true**, response will include Charges array, else, **false**, and charges field will display null

parameters.name: includeLateFees
parameters.description: if **true**, response will include Late Fees array, else, **false**, and late fees field will display as null

parameters.name: includeOwners
parameters.description: if **true**, response will include Owner array, else, **false**, and owners field will display as null

parameters.name: includeDocuments
parameters.description: if **true**, response will include Document array, else, **false**, and documents field will display as null.

parameters.name: includeOwnerTransactions
parameters.description: if **true**, Owner array will populate the Transactions array, else, **false** and includeOwnerTransactions field will display as null

parameters.name: includeOwnerChargeTransactions
parameters.description: if **true**, Owner array will populate the chargeTransactions array, else, **false** and chargeTransactions field will display as null

parameters.name: includeNextAssessment
parameters.description: if **true** Owner array will populate the nextAssessment array, else, **false** and nextAssessment array will display as null

parameters.name: includeOwnerBKList
parameters.description: if **true**, Owner array will populate the bkList array, else, **false** and bkList field will display as null

parameters.name: includeBalanceByChargeType
parameters.description: if **true**, Owner array will populate the balanceByChargeType, else, **false** and balanceByChargeType will display as null.

parameters.name: includePropertyCount
parameters.description: if **true**, response will include propertyCount field, else, **false** and propertyCount field will display as null

parameters.name: includeStartingMonthOfFiscalYear
parameters.description: if **true**, response will include startingMonthOfFiscalYear field, else, **false** and startingMonthOfFiscalYear field will display as null.

parameters.name: includeAssessmentFrequency
parameters.description: if **true**, response will include assessmentFrequencies array, else, **false** and assessmentFrequencies array will display as null

204.description: no content - call was successful, but no data for given parameters

parameters.description: unique identifier for a gl account

parameters.description: beginning date range. *if no startDate is specified, parameter will default to first day of the endDate month.*

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: ending date range. *if no endDate is specified, parameter will default to last day of the startDate month.* **If both startDate and endDate are excluded, default date range will be the previous month.**

ActionCategoryID.description: unique identifier for the action category

created.description: time in UTC ActionItem was created

lastModified.description: time in UTC ActionItem was last modified

actionTypeID.description: uniqueID for the actionType of the requested actionItem

actionStepID.description: uniqueID for the actionStep of the requested actionItem

actionCategoryID.description: uniqueID for the actionCategory of the requested actionItem

assocCode.description: association code for where the requested actionItem belongs

actionItemNotes.description: array of items including created, note, stepID

created.description: time in UTC ActionItem was created

note.example: The following invoice fields were changed: Provider: Post Item, Association:      Condominium Association, Inc., Invoice Date: 2/12/2025, Total: $2,650.00, Pay Type: Misc Check Invoice Detail Added: GL: 203000 - Exchange Account, Fund: Operating, Bank Account: 1026 MAINOPR-7762, Description: Return Deposit Item, Amount: $2,650.00"

actionStepID.description: uniqueID for the actionStep of the requested actionItem

ActionTypeID.description: unique identifier for the action item step

ActionCategoryID.description: unique identifier for the action category

Created.description: date the action item step was created

LastModified.description: date the action item step was created

actionTypeID.description: unique identifier for the Action Type

actionCategoryID.description: unique identifier for the action type category

orderNo.description: unique identifier for each position of the action item's sequence

closedStep.description: if true, closing step, else, false

sendToList.description: list of user types that the action item will be sent to upon stepping

sendToValue.description: array of user types that the action item will be sent to

sendToDescr.description: user role(s) associated with the sendToValue(s)

replyToList.description: list of user types that will receive the action item's replies

replyToValue.description: array of user types that will receive the action item's replies

replyToDescr.description: user role(s) associated with the replyToValue(s)

duration.description: duration to remain at this step

decision.description: user type responsible for decision, if configured. </br> 0 = Not Required </br> 1 = Board Decision </br> 2 - Management Co Decision </br> 3 = Mgmt Co AND Board </br> 4 = Mgmt Co OR Board </br> 5 = Board Vote

yesNextStepID.description: next sequential step the action item will be stepped to if decision = yes

yesNextStepList.description: list of actionStepIDs that the action item can be stepped to if decision = yes

yesNextStepValue.description: array of actionStepIDs that the action item can be stepped to if decision = yes

noNextStepID.description: next sequential step the action item will be stepped to if decision = no

noNextStepList.description: list of actionStepIDs that the action item can be stepped to if decision = no

noNextStepValue.description: array of actionStepIDs that the action item can be stepped to if decision = no

reminderInterval.description: reminder interval of the action item

actionID.description: unique identifer for the additional action item

instructions.description: information entered in the Instruction's section of the action step

blockPayment.description: if true, Block Payments checkbox is enabled on the step's rules, else, false

blockLedger.description: if true, Block Ledger checkbox is enabled on the step's rules, else, false

maskAuthor.description: if true, Hide Sender checkbox is enabled on the step's rules, else, false

holdCollections.description: if true, Hold Collections checkbox is enabled on the step's rules, else, false

holdLateFees.description: if true, Hold Fees checkbox is enabled on the step's rules, else, false

notifyMode.description: Notifications settings 0 = General User Preference </br> 1 = No Notifications </br> 2 = Paper </br> 3 = Email </br> 4 = Electronic (General User Preference) </br> 5 = Paper and Electronic </br> 6 = (Billing User Preference) </br> 7 = Electronic (Billing User Preference) </br> 8 = Electronic Preference, If none, then paper

commMode.description: if true, Send To All checkbox is enabled on the step's rules, else, false

mailAll.description: mailing format 0 = Mailing Address Only </br> 1 = All Addresses - "Mailing Address with Name" tag </br> 2 = All Adresses - "Mailing Aress with Name" tag (Current Resident)

combineAttach.description: if true, Combine Attachments checkbox is enabled on the step's rules, else, false

displayCalendarEventDD.description: Display Calendar Event settings 0 = (Choose a Display Calendar Event) </br> 1 = Do Not Show </br> 2 = Tentative Calendar Event </br> 3 = Confirmed Calendar Event </br> 4 = Remove Calendar Event

displayCalendarEventDDDesc.description: Description associated with the displayCalendarEventDD field

hideFromPortal.description: if true, Hide From Portal checkbox is enabled on the step's rules, else, false

includeInReconPost.description: if true, Include in Reconciliation checkbox is enabled on the step's rules, else, false

CureDate.description: Cure Date of violation action items

HearingDate.description: Hearing Date of violation action items

created.description: date the address was created in Vantaca

isInternational.description: if true, address is international; if false, address is domestic

isPrimaryMailing.description: if true, address is a mailing address; if false, address is not a mailing address

expiration.description: expiration date of the insurance policy

isRequired.description: if **true** insurance policy is required, else **false**

xnNumber.description: unique identifier for an ARC action item

accountNo.description: unique identifier for a single homeowner account

propertyID.description: unique identifier for a single property

hoid.description: unique identifier for a homeowner - homeowners can have one or more accounts/properties

closed.description: if true, item is currently in step marked 'closed', else false

description.example: Your mailbox got knocked off its post.

createdDate.description: date the action item was created

followupDate.description: action item followup date, can be null

dueDate.description: action item due date, can be null

step.description: current step of the action item

lastModified.description: last modified date, can be null

assessment.description: if YES, regular assessment, else NO

specialAssessment.description: if YES, special assessment else NO

finePenalty.description: if YES, fine or penalty, else NO

frequency.description: assessment frequency (for regular or special assessment only)

paidThru.description: For regular and special assessments, the date that the fee is paid through. </br> For delinquent accounts, the oldest date of a transaction

nextDueDate.description: next due date. if the assessment is paid through for the fiscal year, then the first date for the next fiscal year

lastmodified.description: date the assessment was last modified.</br> **if assessment was never modified, field will be null**

assessmentMonth.description: calendar month of the assessment. **For use with annual assessments, monthly assessments will not have an assessmentMonth**

AddressID.description: unique identifier for the address record

lastmodified.description: date-time the address was last modified

attachmentID.description: unique identifier for a single attachment

fileName.description: filename of the attachment with extension

LetterDescription.example: Please resolve the outlined issue by the specified date.

CurePeriod.description: cure period for the CCR item detail

created.description: date the email was added to the owner's account

lastModified.description: date the email was last modified. </br> *if email was never modified, field will be null*

isPrimary.description: if **true**, email address will be used as owner contact, else **false**

bankAccountID.description: unique identifier for the bank account

numberOfUnits.description: number of units within the association

isLive.description: if **true** association is currently Live, else **false**

lastModified.description: date the association was last modified

multipleUnitOwners.description: if units are owned by the same entity(matching name), then **YES**, otherwise **NO**

operatingBalance.description: balance of operating account from the bank register

numberOwnersDelinquent30.description: number of owners with balance over 30 days in regular assessment

delinquencyAmount30.description: total amount of delinquencies over 30 days for regular assessments

numberOwnersDelinquent60.description: number of owners with balance over 60 days in regular assessment

delinquencyAmount60.description: total amount of delinquencies over 60 days for regular assessments

unitsAssessedEquallyAt.description: amount the homeowners are assessed at if all homeowners are assessed at the same amount for regular assessments. If none, field is null

assessmentsLowRange.description: lowest amount that homeowner is assessed at if all homeowners are assessed at different amounts for the regular assessment. If none, field is null

assessmentHighRange.description: highest amount that homeowner is assessed at if all homeowners are assessed at different amounts for the regular assessment. If none, field is null

reserveBudget.description: amount the association has budgeted for reserves

incomeBudget.description: amount the association has budgeted for income

delinquencyDate.description: number of days that an assessment is considered late

interestRate.description: interest percent that would be charged on past due balance for the regular assessment

lateFeeCalculation.description: late fee that would be charged on past due balance for the regular assessment
